The Women of the Mormon Battalion
President Amy Brown Lyman

HE Mormon Battalion was composed of five companies of one hundred men each, all volunteers for their country's service. They were recruited in and about Council Bluffs, and on July 20, 1846, they took up their line of march to Fort Leavenworth, Kansas, headquarters for the army of the West, two hundred miles from Council Bluffs. The order from the Government had stated that each company would be allowed four women as laundresses, who would travel with the company receiving rations and other allowances given to laundresses of the regular army. These five companies were therefore entitled to twenty laundresses, and since this provisi
article_title: The Women of the Mormon Battalion
notes: Found the beginning of the article at the end of the Misc file. The text ends mid-word 'provisi' and connects perfectly with Art 1 which starts 'on offered an opportunity'. Also corrected the OCR error 'HE' to 'THE' and cleaned up the quote mark issue.
